# services.kanata

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.kanata`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.kanata.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable kanata, a tool to improve keyboard comfort and usability with advanced customization. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Keyboard configurations. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.config` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Configuration other than `defcfg`. See [the upstream documentation](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/blob/main/docs/config.adoc) and [example config files](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/tree/main/cfg_samples) for more information. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.configFile` | `absolute path` | The config file. By default, it is generated by values from other kanata module options. You can also set it to your own full config file which overrides all other kanata module options. See [the upstream documentation](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/blob/main/docs/config.adoc) and [example config files](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/tree/main/cfg_samples) for more information. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.devices` | `list of string` | Paths to keyboard devices. An empty list, the default value, lets kanata detect which input devices are keyboards and intercept them all. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Extra command line arguments passed to kanata. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.extraDefCfg` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Configuration of `defcfg` other than `linux-dev` (generated from the devices option) and `linux-continue-if-no-devs-found` (hardcoded to be yes). See [the upstream documentation](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/blob/main/docs/config.adoc) and [example config files](https://github.com/jtroo/kanata/tree/main/cfg_samples) for more information. |
| `services.kanata.keyboards.<name>.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port to run the TCP server on. `null` will not run the server. |
| `services.kanata.package` | `package` | The kanata package to use. ::: {.note} If {option}`danger-enable-cmd` is enabled in any of the keyboards, the `kanata-with-cmd` package should be used. ::: |
